meta.title: F25 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW X3 F25 engine — N20, N52, N55, N47, B47, N57. Real UK costs and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 F25 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F25 engine typically costs £2,200–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B47 20d sits at £2,800–£5,000, while the N57 35d can reach £8,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on engine and specialist location. Evaluate the car's value carefully before committing.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X3 F25 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N55 3.0-litre straight-six petrol is considered one of the most reliable engines in the F25 range — it's a proven, well-understood design. The N52 naturally aspirated six is also exceptionally reliable. The B47 post-LCI diesel is a significant improvement over the N47 [PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Page].

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 F25?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on the N47 diesel and N20 petrol are the most significant engine problems. The N52 and N55 petrols can suffer from water pump failure. The N57 diesel can have EGR cooler and DPF problems. The early xDrive28i is the most reliable of the petrol engines.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X3 F25 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on which engine you choose. The N55 petrol six and N52 naturally aspirated six are exceptionally reliable. The B47 post-LCI diesel is also very good. The N47 diesel and N20 petrol carry the biggest risks — timing chain issues are well-documented.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X3 F25 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is a hard avoid, but the pre-LCI N47 diesel requires very careful consideration — timing chain stretch is well-documented and a full replacement costs £3,200–£5,500 [BMW-QUOTE]. The N20 petrol also has timing chain concerns on early examples. For most buyers, the B47 20d (post-LCI) or N55 35i is the sweet spot.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F25 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle on N47 and N20 engines is the classic early warning of timing chain wear — investigate immediately. On N52/N55 petrols, sudden overheating suggests water pump failure. On N57 diesels, reduced performance or warning lights may indicate EGR cooler or DPF issues.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 F25 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £8,000 F25, a £3,200–£5,500 N47 recon is 40–69% — borderline but potentially viable. Against a tired £5,000 example, the same recon is 64–110% — walk away. Our 70% BMW scrap threshold is the line [BMW-VERIFIED]. For 35i and 35d cars, even the most expensive replacement is viable.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW X3 F25 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N52 and N55 can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing — cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity. The N57 diesel six is also capable of 150,000+ miles. The B47 post-LCI diesel is similarly durable. The N47 and N20 are limited by their timing chain design.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F25 and the E83 X3?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F25 (2010–2017) is the second-generation X3, built on BMW's L6 platform with all-turbocharged engines and improved interior quality. The E83 (2003–2010) was the first generation, built on the E46 platform with naturally aspirated six-cylinder petrols. The F25 was the first X3 built in the USA.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X3 F25 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the engine block. Tell us your variant and production year, and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free. Note that the xDrive28i switched from the N52 (2011–2012) to the N20 (2012–2017).

hero.h1: BMW X3 F25 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the X3 F25 — from the 2.0-litre N20 four-cylinder petrol to the 3.0-litre N55 straight-six and the N57 diesel six. Real UK costs and honest reliability data for BMW's first American-built compact SUV. Backed by 316+ real UK F25 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X3 F25 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the X3 F25 — spanning four-cylinder and six-cylinder petrols, four-cylinder and six-cylinder diesels, and the LCI engine updates.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are [PROVISIONAL] pending dedicated Engine Pages. The N55 straight-six is a proven, reliable engine — it's been in production since 2009 and powers numerous BMW models. The N52 naturally aspirated six (early xDrive28i) is considered one of BMW's most reliable engines. The N20 four-cylinder petrol is generally reliable but timing chain issues have been reported on early examples. The N47 diesel carries the infamous timing chain risk, while the B47 (LCI) is significantly improved. The N57 diesel six is well-regarded for its refinement and durability.

overview.intro: The F25 is the second generation of the BMW X3, unveiled at the 2010 Paris Motor Show and launched in Germany on 20 November 2010. For this generation, BMW moved production from Austria to the Spartanburg plant in South Carolina, USA — the first X3 to be built in America. The F25 brought substantial improvements in design, technology, and the engine lineup, positioning it as a direct rival to the Audi Q5 and Mercedes GLK-Class. The engine range initially offered a 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel (xDrive20d) and a 3.0-litre straight-six petrol (xDrive35i), later expanding to include four-cylinder petrols (N20), six-cylinder diesels (N57), and a rear-wheel-drive sDrive variant introduced in 2012. A significant June 2014 LCI facelift brought the B47 diesel engine, replacing the N47, and updated styling. The F25 was produced until August 2017, succeeded by the third-generation G01 X3.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW L6 (shared with F10 5 Series) • Predecessor: E83 X3 • Successor: G01 X3 • Sibling body styles: None (single SUV body style).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The F25 X3 generated 316+ real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 20d diesel and 35i petrol as the range reaches affordable used-car territory.

items.quote: BMW's N55 single-turbo straight-six is a masterpiece — smooth, responsive, and with a strong torque curve from 1,200 rpm. The xDrive35i offers effortless performance and the unmistakable six-cylinder soundtrack that defines BMW's petrol heritage.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six-cylinder performance and refinement.

items.quote: The N57 straight-six diesel is a masterpiece of refinement and efficiency — 258 bhp, 560 Nm of torque, and genuine 40+ mpg capability. The xDrive30d offers the best blend of performance and economy in the F25 diesel range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want effortless torque and long-distance cruising capability.

items.quote: The rear-mounted timing chain is the defining risk of the N47 — cold-start rattle is the warning, and a snapped chain means full replacement. The N47 remains the biggest single risk in the F25 range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers on a budget who must have documented chain-replacement history.

items.quote: The twin-turbo N57 offers spectacular performance — 313 bhp and 630 Nm of torque — but the additional turbocharger adds complexity and potential failure points. A full replacement can exceed £8,000.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most powerful diesel F25 and have budget for potential turbocharger repairs.

items.quote: The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47 — timing chain issues are far less frequent, and the engine is smoother and more refined. The post-LCI 20d offers the best blend of economy and reliability in the F25 range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel economy without the N47's timing chain anxiety.

items.quote: The N52-powered xDrive28i was only available from 2011–2012 before being replaced by the turbocharged N20 — but it's a gem. No turbos, no HPFP, just smooth, linear six-cylinder power with VANOS as the primary wear item. It's the last of BMW's great naturally aspirated straight-sixes in the X3.
items.whoItsFor: enthusiasts who want the simplicity of a naturally aspirated six.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Petrol — reliable naturally aspirated six.
High Risk — rear chain defines this engine.
Solid Choice — significantly improved over N47.

economicsRule.title: The F25 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,200–£5,500 N47 recon against a £4,000–£15,000 F25 sits at 21–138% of the car's value【EM-VERIFIED】. On a sub-£5,000 example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £8,000+ example, the same recon drops to 40–69% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first. For 35i and 35d cars, the higher retained values make replacement more viable.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F25 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B47 20d (post-LCI) — efficient, reliable, and cheaper to maintain than the six-cylinder diesels.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F25 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: N57 35d — twin-turbo complexity and six-cylinder running costs add up.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F25 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: N55 35i — six-cylinder refinement, strong performance, and proven reliability.

problems.title: N47 Diesel Timing Chain Stretch (Pre-LCI)
problems.affectedModels: F25 sDrive18d, xDrive20d, xDrive28d (2010–2014, N47 engine).
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain receives the last oil feed and is tensioned by a plastic guide that degrades over time — cold-start rattle for 1–2 seconds is the classic early warning. This is the defining weakness of BMW's N47 diesel family.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold-start rattle on any N47 diesel F25, get the chain inspected immediately — a £650–£1,800 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,200–£5,500 replacement that follows if ignored. On an F25 worth under £5,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable.

cta.label: Compare N47 diesel engine prices for your F25 →

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain & VANOS Issues
problems.affectedModels: F25 sDrive20i, xDrive20i, xDrive28i (2011–2017, N20 engine).
problems.rootCause: The N20 four-cylinder turbo petrol can suffer from timing chain tensioner wear and VANOS solenoid failure. The chain is located at the front of the engine, making replacement less labour-intensive than the N47, but failure is still catastrophic if ignored.

tieredCosts.work: Chain, tensioner and VANOS solenoid replacement

problems.recommendation: Have the timing chain and VANOS system inspected at 60,000 miles — a £700–£1,500 preventative service is far cheaper than the £3,500–£5,500 replacement that follows a catastrophic failure. Early N20s are the highest risk.

cta.label: Compare N20 engine prices for your F25 →

problems.title: N52/N55 Water Pump & Cooling System Failure
problems.rootCause: The electric water pump on N52 and N55 engines is a known weak point — failure leads to sudden overheating and potential engine damage. This is a common issue across BMW's six-cylinder petrol range.

tieredCosts.work: Full engine replacement if overheating caused damage

problems.recommendation: Replace the water pump as preventative maintenance at 60,000 miles — a £400–£800 specialist job is trivial against the £3,500+ engine replacement that follows an ignored overheating event.

cta.label: Compare N52/N55 engine prices for your F25 →

problems.title: N57 Diesel EGR Cooler & Emission System Issues
problems.rootCause: The N57 diesel's EGR cooler can develop internal leaks, allowing coolant to enter the exhaust system. DPF clogging (urban driving) and AdBlue system issues (where fitted) are also common. BMW has issued recalls for EGR cooler issues on some models.

tieredCosts.work: EGR cooler replacement, DPF clean, AdBlue system service
Full engine replacement if coolant contamination caused damage

problems.recommendation: Check your N57 F25's VIN against any outstanding EGR cooler recalls — if affected, the repair is free. For DPF health, ensure regular motorway driving to allow regeneration cycles to complete. Have the emission system inspected at 60,000 miles.

cta.label: Compare N57 diesel engine prices for your F25 →

replacementCosts.h2: F25 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the EM_Proprietary_Data baseline figures — F25 X3 shares the L6 platform with the F10 5 Series, with similar engine-bay access, so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are F25 X3 only. xDrive (all-wheel drive) was standard at launch; sDrive (rear-wheel drive) was introduced in 2012 for some markets. The xDrive28i used the N52B30 (3.0L naturally aspirated) from 2011–2012, then switched to the N20B20 (2.0L turbo) from 2012 onward. The LCI (2014–2017) introduced the B47 diesel engine, replacing the N47 in 18d and 20d models.

items.question: How much does a BMW X3 F25 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned F25 engine typically costs £2,200–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B47 20d sits at £2,800–£5,000, while the N57 35d can reach £8,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on engine and specialist location. Evaluate the car's value carefully before committing.

items.question: Which BMW X3 F25 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The N55 3.0-litre straight-six petrol (xDrive35i) is considered one of the most reliable engines in the F25 range — it's a proven, well-understood design. The N52 naturally aspirated six (early xDrive28i) is also exceptionally reliable. The B47 post-LCI diesel is a significant improvement over the N47 [PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Page].

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 F25?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch on the N47 diesel (pre-LCI 18d/20d) and N20 petrol (20i/28i) are the most significant engine problems. The N52 and N55 petrols can suffer from water pump failure. The N57 diesel can have EGR cooler and DPF problems. The early xDrive28i (N52) is the most reliable of the petrol engines.

items.question: Is the BMW X3 F25 reliable?
items.answer: It depends heavily on which engine you choose. The N55 petrol six and N52 naturally aspirated six are exceptionally reliable. The B47 post-LCI diesel is also very good. The N47 diesel and N20 petrol carry the biggest risks — timing chain issues are well-documented. The N57 diesel six is reliable but requires careful attention to emission system maintenance.

items.question: Which BMW X3 F25 engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine is a hard avoid, but the pre-LCI N47 diesel (sDrive18d, xDrive20d, xDrive28d) requires very careful consideration — timing chain stretch is well-documented and a full replacement costs £3,200–£5,500 [BMW-QUOTE]. The N20 petrol also has timing chain concerns on early examples. For most buyers, the B47 20d (post-LCI) or N55 35i is the sweet spot.

items.question: How do I know if my F25 engine needs replacing?
items.answer: Cold-start rattle on N47 and N20 engines is the classic early warning of timing chain wear — investigate immediately. On N52/N55 petrols, sudden overheating suggests water pump failure. On N57 diesels, reduced performance or warning lights may indicate EGR cooler or DPF issues. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 F25 engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £8,000 F25, a £3,200–£5,500 N47 recon is 40–69% — borderline but potentially viable. Against a tired £5,000 example, the same recon is 64–110% — walk away. Our 70% BMW scrap threshold is the line [BMW-VERIFIED]. For 35i and 35d cars (£10,000–£18,000+), even the most expensive replacement is viable.

items.question: How long do BMW X3 F25 engines last?
items.answer: The N52 and N55 can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing — cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity. The N57 diesel six is also capable of 150,000+ miles with careful maintenance. The B47 post-LCI diesel is similarly durable. The N47 and N20 are limited by their timing chain design — addressed proactively, either can reach 120,000+ miles, but many fail earlier without intervention.

items.question: What is the difference between the F25 and the E83 X3?
items.answer: The F25 (2010–2017) is the second-generation X3, built on BMW's L6 platform with all-turbocharged engines and improved interior quality. The E83 (2003–2010) was the first generation, built on the E46 3 Series platform with naturally aspirated six-cylinder petrols and the M57 diesel. The F25 is larger, more refined, and features more advanced technology. The F25 was also the first X3 built in the USA (Spartanburg, South Carolina).

items.question: How do I find my BMW X3 F25 engine code?
items.answer: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the engine block. Tell us your variant (sDrive20i, xDrive20i, xDrive28i, xDrive35i, sDrive18d, xDrive20d, xDrive28d, xDrive30d, xDrive35d), production year, and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free. Note that the xDrive28i switched from the N52 (2011–2012) to the N20 (2012–2017).
